lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Hi, my name's Cathy, nice to meet you.
lines.translation: 嗨，我叫凯茜，很高兴认识你。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Nice to meet you too Kathy, my name's John.
lines.translation: 我也很高兴认识你，凯茜，我叫约翰。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: I'm a university friend of the bride.
lines.translation: 我是新娘的大学同学。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: What about you?
lines.translation: 你呢？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Who do you know at this party?
lines.translation: 这个聚会上你认识谁？

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: I am a colleague of Brenda.
lines.translation: 我是布伦达的同事。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: I was a little surprised to be invited to be honest.
lines.translation: 老实说，收到邀请我有点惊讶。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: We've only been working together the last six months, but we quickly became good friends.
lines.translation: 我们才一起工作了六个月，但很快就成了好朋友。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: We just wrapped up a project with a difficult client last week.
lines.translation: 我们上周刚完成了一个棘手客户的项目。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: I bet Brenda is glad it's done with, and she can focus on wedding preparations.
lines.translation: 我敢说布伦达一定很高兴项目结束了，这样她就可以专注于筹备婚礼了。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Oh, yes.
lines.translation: 噢，是的。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: So you are Cathy from the office.
lines.translation: 所以你就是办公室里的那个凯茜。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Actually I've heard a lot about you in that project, the client sounded like a real nightmare.
lines.translation: 实际上，我也听说了很多关于你在那个项目中的事，那个客户听起来简直是个噩梦。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Oh, he was, I mean we deal with all kinds of people on a regular basis, it's part of the job, but he was especially particular.
lines.translation: 噢，他确实是。我是说，我们经常要和各种各样的人打交道，这是工作的一部分，但他尤其挑剔。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Enough about that, what line of work are you in?
lines.translation: 不说那个了，你是做什么工作的？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Well, right out of college I worked in advertising for a while.
lines.translation: 嗯，大学刚毕业时我在广告公司工作过一段时间。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Recently though, I turn my photography hobby into a small business.
lines.translation: 不过最近，我把摄影爱好变成了一门小生意。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: I'll actually be taking photos during the big event as a wedding gift.
lines.translation: 实际上，我会作为婚礼礼物在大日子那天负责拍照。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: That sounds wonderful and very thoughtful of you.
lines.translation: 听起来真不错，你考虑得真周到。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: I bake, just as a hobby.
lines.translation: 我烘焙，只是作为爱好。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: But Brenda has asked me to do the cake for the wedding.
lines.translation: 但布伦达请我为婚礼做蛋糕。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: I was a bit nervous saying yes because I'm far from a professional.
lines.translation: 答应的时候我有点紧张，因为我远非专业人士。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Did you bake the cookies here at the party tonight?
lines.translation: 今晚聚会上的这些饼干是你烤的吗？

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Yes, I got the idea from a magazine.
lines.translation: 是的，我从杂志上得到的灵感。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: They're delicious!
lines.translation: 它们真好吃！

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: You've got nothing to worry about.
lines.translation: 你完全不用担心。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: You are a natural.
lines.translation: 你有天赋。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: You really think so?
lines.translation: 你真的这么认为吗？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: If you hadn't told me that, I would have guessed they were baked by the restaurant.
lines.translation: 如果你没告诉我，我还会以为这些是餐厅烤的呢。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: You know, with your event planning experience you could very well open your own shop.
lines.translation: 你知道的，凭你的活动策划经验，你完全可以开一家自己的店。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: (laughing) One step at a time.
lines.translation: （笑）一步步来吧。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: First, I'll see how baking the wedding cake goes.
lines.translation: 首先，我得看看婚礼蛋糕做得怎么样。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: If it's not a disaster, maybe I'll give it some more thought.
lines.translation: 如果没搞砸的话，也许我会再考虑考虑。

lines.speaker: Q1.
lines.text: What did Cathy and Brenda finish doing last week?
lines.translation: 凯茜和布伦达上周完成了什么工作？

lines.speaker: Q2.
lines.text: What is John going to do for Brenda?
lines.translation: 约翰打算为布伦达做什么？

lines.speaker: Q3.
lines.text: How did Cathy feel when asked to bake the cake?
lines.translation: 当被要求做蛋糕时，凯茜感觉如何？

lines.speaker: Q4.
lines.text: What does the man suggest the woman do?
lines.translation: 这位男士建议这位女士做什么？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: You are heading for a completely different world, now that you are about to graduate from high school.
lines.translation: 既然你即将从高中毕业，你正走向一个完全不同的世界。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: I know it's the end of high school, but many of classmates are going on to the same university, and we are still required to study hard.
lines.translation: 我知道高中生活结束了，但我很多同学都要去同一所大学，我们仍然被要求努力学习。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: So what's the difference?
lines.translation: 那有什么不同呢？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Many aspects are different here at the university.
lines.translation: 在大学里，很多方面都不同。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: The most important one is that you have to take more individual responsibility for your actions.
lines.translation: 最重要的一点是，你必须为自己的行为承担更多的个人责任。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: It's up to your own self-discipline—how much efforts you put into study.
lines.translation: 学习投入多少精力全靠你的自律。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Living in college dormitories, there are no parents to tell you that study harder or stop wasting time.
lines.translation: 住在大学宿舍里，没有父母会告诉你要努力学习或不要浪费时间。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Lectures have hundreds of students and they are not going to follow you up or question you if you miss the lectures.
lines.translation: 大课有数百名学生，如果你缺课，他们也不会跟踪你或质问你。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Nobody cares you mean?
lines.translation: 你的意思是没人管吗？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: It's not that nobody concerned about you, it's just that suddenly at the university you are expected to behave like an adult.
lines.translation: 不是没人关心你，只是在大学里，你突然被期待表现得像个成年人一样。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: That means concentrating on the direction of your life in general and your own academic performance specifically.
lines.translation: 这意味着要专注于你人生的大致方向，特别是你自己的学术表现。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: For example…?
lines.translation: 例如……？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Well, like you need to manage daily, weekly and monthly schedules, so that you study regularly.
lines.translation: 嗯，比如你需要管理每日、每周和每月的日程表，这样你才能有规律地学习。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Be sure to attend all classes and leave enough time to finish your assignments and prepare well for examinations.
lines.translation: 一定要参加所有的课程，留出足够的时间完成作业并为考试做好充分准备。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Ok, and what else is different?
lines.translation: 好的，还有什么不同？

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Well, in college there are lots of distractions, and you need to control yourself.
lines.translation: 嗯，在大学里有很多诱惑，你需要控制好自己。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: You will make interesting friends, but you need only keep the friends who respect your students' commitments.
lines.translation: 你会结交有趣的朋友，但你只需要保留那些尊重你学生职责的朋友。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: Also, there are a lot of wonderful clubs, but you shouldn't allocate too much time to club activities, unless they are directly related to your study.
lines.translation: 此外，还有很多精彩的社团，但除非它们与你的学习直接相关，否则你不应该在社团活动上分配太多时间。

lines.speaker: M:
lines.text: It's also your choice if you want to go out at night, but you will be foolish to let that affect class performance during the day.
lines.translation: 晚上想出去玩也是你的选择，但如果你让这影响到白天的课堂表现，那就太愚蠢了。

lines.speaker: W:
lines.text: Well, I'm determined to do well at the university and I guess I'm going to have to grow up fast.
lines.translation: 嗯，我决心在大学里表现出色，我想我得快点成长起来了。

lines.speaker: Q5.
lines.text: What does the man say about college students as compared with high schoolers?
lines.translation: 关于大学生与高中生的比较，这位男士说了什么？

lines.speaker: Q6.
lines.text: What are college students expected to do according to the man?
lines.translation: 根据这位男士的说法，大学生被期待做什么？

lines.speaker: Q7.
lines.text: What kind of friends does the man suggest the woman make as a college student?
lines.translation: 这位男士建议这位女士作为大学生应该交什么样的朋友？

lines.speaker: Q8.
lines.text: What kind of club activities should college students engage in according to the man?
lines.translation: 根据这位男士的说法，大学生应该参加什么样的社团活动？

lines.text: Most successful people are unorthodox persons whose minds wonder outside traditional ways of thinking.
lines.translation: 大多数成功人士都是非传统的人，他们的思维游走在传统思维方式之外。

lines.text: Instead of trying to refine old formulas, they invent new ones.
lines.translation: 他们不是试图完善旧公式，而是发明新公式。

lines.text: When Jean-Claude Killy made the French national ski team in the early 1960s, he was prepared to work harder than anyone else to be the best.
lines.translation: 20世纪60年代初，当让-克洛德·基利入选法国国家滑雪队时，他准备比任何人都更努力，以成为最优秀的人。

lines.text: At the crack of dawn, he would run up the slopes with his skis on, an unbelievably backbreaking activity.
lines.translation: 黎明时分，他会带着滑雪板跑上山坡，这是一项令人难以置信的、极其辛苦的活动。

lines.text: In the evening, he would do weightlifting and running.
lines.translation: 晚上，他会练习举重和跑步。

lines.text: But the other team members were working as hard and long as he was.
lines.translation: 但其他队员和他一样努力，工作时间也一样长。

lines.text: He realized instinctively that simply training harder would never be enough.
lines.translation: 他本能地意识到，仅仅更努力地训练是永远不够的。

lines.text: Killy then began challenging the basic theories of racing technique.
lines.translation: 于是，基利开始挑战竞赛技术的基本理论。

lines.text: Each week, he would try something different to see if he could find a better, faster way down the mountain.
lines.translation: 每周，他都会尝试不同的方法，看看能否找到一种更好、更快的下山方式。

lines.text: His experiments resulted in a new style that was almost exactly opposite the accepted technique of the time.
lines.translation: 他的实验产生了一种全新的风格，几乎与当时公认的技术完全相反。

lines.text: It involved skiing with his legs apart for better balance and sitting back on the skis when he came to a turn.
lines.translation: 这包括滑雪时两腿分开以保持更好的平衡，并在转弯时重心后移。

lines.text: He also used ski poles in an unorthodox way--to propel himself as he skied.
lines.translation: 他还以一种非传统的方式使用滑雪杖——在滑雪时推动自己前进。

lines.text: The explosive new style helped cut Killy's racing time dramatically.
lines.translation: 这种爆发性的新风格帮助基利大幅缩短了比赛时间。

lines.text: In 1966 and 1967, he captured virtually every major skiing trophy.
lines.translation: 1966年和1967年，他几乎夺得了所有主要的滑雪奖杯。

lines.text: The next year, he won three gold medals in the Winter Olympics, a record in ski racing that has never been topped.
lines.translation: 次年，他在冬奥会上获得了三枚金牌，这一纪录在滑雪比赛中至今未被超越。

lines.text: Killy learned an important secret shared by many creative people: innovations don't require genius, just a willingness to question the way things have always been done.
lines.translation: 基利学会了一个许多有创意的人共有的重要秘密：创新不需要天才，只需要愿意去质疑一直以来的做法。

lines.speaker: Q9.
lines.text: What does the speaker say about most successful people?
lines.translation: 发言者关于大多数成功人士说了什么？

lines.speaker: Q10.
lines.text: What does the speaker say about Killy's experiments?
lines.translation: 发言者关于基利的实验说了什么？

lines.speaker: Q11.
lines.text: What is said to be Killy's biggest honor in his skiing career?
lines.translation: 什么是基利滑雪生涯中最大的荣誉？

lines.text: Scientific experiments have demonstrated incredible ways to kill a guinea pig, a small furry animal.
lines.translation: 科学实验展示了杀死豚鼠（一种毛茸茸的小动物）的令人难以置信的方法。

lines.text: Emotional upsets generate powerful and deadly toxic substances.
lines.translation: 情绪波动会产生强大且致命的有毒物质。

lines.text: Blood samples taken from persons experiencing intense fear or anger when injected into guinea pigs have killed them in less than two minutes.
lines.translation: 从正处于极度恐惧或愤怒中的人身上提取的血样，在注入豚鼠体内后，不到两分钟就杀死了它们。

lines.text: Imagine what these poisonous substances can do to your own body.
lines.translation: 想象一下这些有毒物质会对你自己的身体造成什么影响。

lines.text: Every thought that you have affects your body chemistry within a split second.
lines.translation: 你的每一个想法都会在瞬间影响你的身体化学反应。

lines.text: Remember how you feel when you are speeding down the highway and a big truck suddenly brakes twenty meters in front of you.
lines.translation: 回想一下，当你正在高速公路上疾驰，一辆大卡车突然在前方二十米处刹车时的感觉。

lines.text: A shock wave shoots through your whole system.
lines.translation: 一股冲击波会席卷你的整个系统。

lines.text: Your mind produces instant reactions in your body.
lines.translation: 你的思想会引起身体的瞬间反应。

lines.text: The toxic substances that fear, anger, frustration and stress produce not only kill guinea pigs but kill us off in a similar manner.
lines.translation: 恐惧、愤怒、沮丧和压力产生的有毒物质不仅能杀死豚鼠，还会以类似的方式置我们于死地。

lines.text: It is impossible to be fearful, anxious, irritated and healthy at the same time.
lines.translation: 一个人不可能在充满恐惧、焦虑、恼火的同时又保持健康。

lines.text: It is not just difficult, it is impossible.
lines.translation: 这不仅仅是困难，而是不可能。

lines.text: Simply put, your body's health is a reflection of your mental health.
lines.translation: 简单地说，你的身体健康是你心理健康的反映。

lines.text: Sickness will often then be a result of unresolved inner conflicts which in time show up in the body.
lines.translation: 疾病通常是未解决的内在冲突的结果，这些冲突随着时间的推移会表现在身体上。

lines.text: It is also fascinating how our subconscious mind shapes our health.
lines.translation: 我们的潜意识如何塑造我们的健康也同样引人入胜。

lines.text: Do you recall falling sick on a day when you didn't want to go to school?
lines.translation: 你还记得在不想去学校的那天生病的事吗？

lines.text: Headaches brought on by fear?
lines.translation: 恐惧引起的头痛？

lines.text: The mind-body connection is such that if, for example, we want to avoid something, very often our subconscious mind will arrange it.
lines.translation: 身心联系是如此紧密，以至于例如如果我们想避开某件事，我们的潜意识往往会做出安排。

lines.text: Once we recognize that these things happen to us, we are half way to doing something about them.
lines.translation: 一旦我们意识到这些事情发生在我们身上，我们就已经成功了一半去解决它们。

lines.speaker: Q12.
lines.text: What happens to guinea pigs when blood samples of angry people are injected into them?
lines.translation: 当愤怒的人的血样被注入豚鼠体内时，会发生什么？

lines.speaker: Q13.
lines.text: What does the speaker say about every thought you have?
lines.translation: 发言者关于你的每一个想法说了什么？

lines.speaker: Q14.
lines.text: What does the speaker say is impossible?
lines.translation: 发言者说什么是不可可能的？

lines.speaker: Q15.
lines.text: What does the passage say about our mind and body?
lines.translation: 短文关于我们的思想和身体说了什么？

lines.text: Teachers and students alike have experienced the curious paradox that beginners, as a rule, tend to think too little about what they are doing because they think too much about what they are doing.
lines.translation: 教师和学生都经历过一个奇怪的悖论：通常情况下，初学者往往对自己在做什么思考得太少，因为他们对自己在做什么思考得太多。

lines.text: Take for example people who are learning to play basketball or the piano.
lines.translation: 以学习打篮球或弹钢琴的人为例。

lines.text: They have to give so much thought and attention to the low-level mechanics of handling the ball or fingering the keys or reading the music, that they are unable to give any thought to the thing that matters – the game, or the music, respectively.
lines.translation: 他们不得不把如此多的心思和注意力放在控球、按键或识谱等低层次的技巧上，以至于无法去思考真正重要的事情——分别是比赛或音乐。

lines.text: With experts, it's just the other way around.
lines.translation: 对于专家来说，情况恰恰相反。

lines.text: They are open to the tactical possibilities and the musical challenges precisely because they are freed, through skill, from the need to pay attention to the low-level details of how to play.
lines.translation: 他们能接受战术上的可能性和音乐上的挑战，正是因为通过技能，他们从关注如何演奏的低层次细节中解放了出来。

lines.text: Indeed, when the expert pays attention to the mechanics, this is liable to disrupt performance.
lines.translation: 事实上，当专家关注技巧时，这反而容易破坏表现。

lines.text: This has led some to say that the expert operates in a zone 'beyond thought', in a state of flow.
lines.translation: 这导致一些人说专家是在一种“超越思维”的区域运行，处于一种“心流”状态。

lines.text: But this is misleading.
lines.translation: 但这具有误导性。

lines.text: Expert performance is not beyond thought.
lines.translation: 专家的表现并非超越了思维。

lines.text: Smart basketball players or skilled musicians need to pay close attention to the demands of high performance, to the challenges to be overcome.
lines.translation: 聪明的篮球运动员或熟练的音乐家需要密切关注高水平表现的要求，关注需要克服的挑战。

lines.text: What they don't need to do – what would be a distraction – is to have to think about where their fingers are, or how to control the ball while running.
lines.translation: 他们不需要做的事情——这反而会分散注意力——是去思考手指的位置，或者如何在跑步时控球。

lines.text: It's not mechanics, but the play itself, that absorbs the experts' intelligence.
lines.translation: 吸引专家才智的不是技巧，而是比赛或演奏本身。

lines.text: A nice video published online last month sheds light on expertise and the conscious mind.
lines.translation: 上个月在网上发布的一段精彩视频阐明了专长和意识。

lines.text: The video reports a new study using an eye-tracking device.
lines.translation: 该视频报道了一项使用眼动追踪设备的新研究。

lines.text: It turns out that the less-skilled pianist spends more time looking at her fingers than does the expert who, in contrast, is more likely to be looking at the sheet music, or looking ahead at keys he's not yet playing.
lines.translation: 结果显示，技能较低的钢琴家花在看手指上的时间比专家多，而相比之下，专家更有可能在看乐谱，或者看向他尚未演奏的琴键。

lines.text: In general, the expert's gaze was calmer and more stable.
lines.translation: 总的来说，专家的目光更从容、更稳定。

lines.text: This is not a surprising finding.
lines.translation: 这并不是一个令人惊讶的发现。

lines.text: It supports what we might almost think of as conventional wisdom.
lines.translation: 它支持了我们几乎可以认为是常识的观点。

lines.text: But it's remarkable for all that, nonetheless.
lines.translation: 尽管如此，这仍然是引人注目的。

lines.text: The eye tracker gives expert and learning performers a glimpse into what they do without thinking about it.
lines.translation: 眼动追踪仪让专家和学习型表演者得以一窥他们在不加思索的情况下所做的事情。

lines.text: The topic of the nature of skill – and the differences between beginners and experts – has been one of considerable discussion in cognitive science and philosophy.
lines.translation: 技能的本质——以及初学者和专家之间的区别——一直是认知科学和哲学中广泛讨论的话题之一。

lines.speaker: Q16.
lines.text: What does the speaker say about beginners and expert pianists?
lines.translation: 发言者关于初学者和专家级钢琴家说了什么？

lines.speaker: Q17.
lines.text: What do smart basketball players do according to the speaker?
lines.translation: 根据发言者的说法，聪明的篮球运动员会做什么？

lines.speaker: Q18.
lines.text: What do we learn about the new study published in an online video?
lines.translation: 关于在线视频中发布的新研究，我们了解到了什么？

lines.text: Every summer when I top up my selection of summer outfits from the department stores, my eyes would nearly pop out of my head.
lines.translation: 每年夏天，当我从百货公司选购夏季服装时，我的眼睛都快跳出来了。

lines.text: I'm overwhelmed with the wide range of different slimming products each year.
lines.translation: 每年我都会被琳琅满目的各种减肥产品所淹没。

lines.text: And more shockingly, these products are often advocated by very slim models.
lines.translation: 更令人震惊的是，这些产品通常由非常苗条的模特代言。

lines.text: Having lived in Asia for almost ten years now, I've seen various dieting tips come and go.
lines.translation: 在亚洲生活了将近十年，我见识了各种各样的节食秘诀盛极一时又销声匿迹。

lines.text: I remember in Japan people heading directly to the food section in the supermarket when the banana diet was at its peak.
lines.translation: 我记得在日本，“香蕉减肥法”盛行时，人们会直接冲向超市的食品区。

lines.text: Then, there was the black tea and oolong tea diet followed by the soybean diet and the tomato juice diet.
lines.translation: 然后，是红茶和乌龙茶减肥法，接着是黄豆减肥法和番茄汁减肥法。

lines.text: The list goes on and on.
lines.translation: 名单越拉越长。

lines.text: Apart from what people eat, I've also seen many interesting slimming products.
lines.translation: 除了人们吃的东西，我还见过许多有趣的减肥产品。

lines.text: In Hong Kong, I've seen girls wrapping their whole body or both legs up with a special type of slimming tape which is supposed to help make them thinner.
lines.translation: 在香港，我见过女孩子用一种特殊的减肥胶带把全身或双腿缠起来，据说这能帮助她们变瘦。

lines.text: But it just reminded me of the roasted ham my mother usually puts on the dinner table of Christmas.
lines.translation: 但这只让我想起我母亲通常在圣诞晚餐桌上放的烤火腿。

lines.text: Then there were the face slimming rollers that were said to improve your blood circulation and make your face smaller.
lines.translation: 然后还有瘦脸按摩仪，据说能促进血液循环，让脸变小。

lines.text: Personally, I do not believe in any of these slimming gadgets.
lines.translation: 就个人而言，我不相信任何这些减肥小玩意。

lines.text: And I think I have a very different perspective when it comes to the definition of what is beautiful.
lines.translation: 我认为在对美的定义上，我有一个非常不同的视角。

lines.text: Asian women prefer to avoid the sun because being pale or white is considered beautiful, whereas a tanned complexion is considered much more beautiful and sexy in the west.
lines.translation: 亚洲女性更喜欢躲避阳光，因为皮肤白皙被认为是美的，而在西方，晒黑的肤色被认为更美、更性感。

lines.text: It is most certainly shaped by a person's culture as well as how they were raised in their childhood.
lines.translation: 这当然是由一个人的文化以及他们童年时期的成长方式塑造的。

lines.text: As each summer season approaches, there's no escape from it.
lines.translation: 每当夏季临近，就无法逃避这个话题。

lines.text: But it's not only women who are affected by this pressure to look good.
lines.translation: 但不仅女性受到这种变美压力的影响。

lines.text: Men aspire to be able to show off their six packs or their V-shape backs and there's a growing market of slimming pills aimed at men too.
lines.translation: 男性渴望能炫耀他们的六块腹肌或V型背部，针对男性的减肥药市场也在不断增长。

lines.text: I think no matter what diets we follow or what slimming products we obsess ourselves with, at the end of the day there's no magic trick to shape up for the summer.
lines.translation: 我认为，无论我们遵循什么样的饮食，或者沉迷于什么样的减肥产品，到头来，夏季塑形并没有什么魔术般的诀窍。

lines.text: Eat in a balance way and incorporate the right level of physical activity.
lines.translation: 均衡饮食并加入适量的体育活动。

lines.text: For me, this still seems to be the best plan.
lines.translation: 对我来说，这似乎仍然是最好的计划。

lines.speaker: Q19.
lines.text: What overwhelms the speaker when she buys her summer outfits each year?
lines.translation: 每年购买夏季服装时，什么让发言者感到压力？

lines.speaker: Q20.
lines.text: What does the speaker think of girls wrapping their legs up with slimming tape?
lines.translation: 发言者如何看待用减肥胶带缠腿的女孩？

lines.speaker: Q21.
lines.text: What does the speaker think affects people's interpretation of beauty?
lines.translation: 发言者认为什么会影响人们对美的诠释？

lines.text: Skin may seem like a superficial human attribute, but it is the first thing we notice about anyone we meet.
lines.translation: 皮肤看起来似乎是人类的一种表面属性，但它是我们见到任何人时首先注意到的东西。

lines.text: As a zoologist focusing on the studies of apes and monkeys, I've been studying why humans evolved to become the naked ape, and why skin comes in so many different shades around the world.
lines.translation: 作为一名专注于猿猴研究的动物学家，我一直在研究为什么人类进化成了“裸猿”，以及为什么世界各地的人皮肤色调如此不同。

lines.text: We can make a very good estimate from the fossil record that humans probably evolved naked skin around a million and a half years ago.
lines.translation: 我们可以从化石记录中做一个很好的估计：人类大约在150万年前进化出了裸露的皮肤。

lines.text: And meanwhile, they mostly lost their coat of fur.
lines.translation: 与此同时，他们大多失去了皮毛。

lines.text: Today, we have a few patches of hair remaining on various parts of our bodies.
lines.translation: 今天，我们身体的各个部位还保留着一些毛发。

lines.text: But compared with apes and monkeys, we have very little.
lines.translation: 但与猿和猴子相比，我们的毛发非常少。

lines.text: Basically, we turned our skin darker to serve as a natural sun-protector in the place of the hair we lost.
lines.translation: 基本上，我们将皮肤变黑，以作为一种天然的防晒保护，取代我们失去的毛发。

lines.text: We think we lost this hair because of the need to keep ourselves cool, when we were moving around vigorously in a hot environment.
lines.translation: 我们认为我们失去这些毛发是因为在炎热的环境中剧烈运动时需要保持凉爽。

lines.text: We can't really lose heat by breathing quickly and loudly like dogs.
lines.translation: 我们不能像狗那样通过快速大声呼吸来散热。

lines.text: We have to do it by sweating.
lines.translation: 我们必须通过出汗来散热。

lines.text: So we evolved the ability to sweat plentifully, and lost most of our fur.
lines.translation: 于是我们进化出了大量出汗的能力，并失去了大部分皮毛。

lines.text: Most animals protect themselves from the sun with fur.
lines.translation: 大多数动物用皮毛保护自己免受阳光照射。

lines.text: What we did in our ancestry was to produce more permanent natural coloring in our skin cells.
lines.translation: 我们的祖先所做的就是在皮肤细胞中产生更持久的天然色素。

lines.text: This was really an important revolution in human history, because it allowed us to continue to evolve in equatorial environments.
lines.translation: 这确实是人类历史上的一次重要革命，因为它使我们能够在赤道环境中继续进化。

lines.text: It really made it possible for us to continue along the path toward modern humans in Africa.
lines.translation: 它确实使我们在非洲继续沿着通往现代人类的道路前进成为可能。

lines.text: For most of the human history, we all had dark skin.
lines.translation: 在人类历史的大部分时间里，我们都是黑皮肤。

lines.text: What we see today is the product of evolutionary events, resulting from the dispersal of a few human populations out of Africa around 60,000 to 70,000 years ago.
lines.translation: 我们今天所看到的进化事件的产物，是大约6万到7万年前少数人群离开非洲向外扩散的结果。

lines.text: Our species originated around 200,000 years ago, and underwent tremendous diversification, culturally, technologically, linguistically, artistically, for 130,000 years.
lines.translation: 我们的物种起源于大约20万年前，并经历了13万年的巨大多样化，包括文化、技术、语言和艺术。

lines.text: After that, a few small populations left Africa to populate the rest of the world.
lines.translation: 在那之后，一小部分人群离开非洲，到世界其他地方定居。

lines.text: These early ancestors of modern Eurasians disperse into parts of the world that had more seasonal sunshine and much lower levels of sun radiation.
lines.translation: 现代欧亚人的这些早期祖先分散到世界上季节性阳光充足且阳光辐射水平低得多的地区。

lines.text: It's in these populations that we begin to see real changes in the genetic makeup of natural coloring.
lines.translation: 正是在这些人群中，我们开始看到天然色素遗传构成的真实变化。

lines.text: Today, skin color is evolving via new mixtures of people coming together and having children with new mixtures of skin color genes.
lines.translation: 今天，肤色正通过人们聚集在一起并产生带有新肤色基因组合的后代而不断演变。

lines.text: We can see this in almost every large city worldwide.
lines.translation: 我们几乎在世界各地的每个大城市都能看到这种情况。

lines.text: Not only the coloring genes, but lots of other genes are getting mixed up, too.
lines.translation: 不仅是色素基因，许多其他基因也正在混合。

lines.speaker: Q22.
lines.text: What does the speaker mainly talk about?
lines.translation: 发言者主要谈论什么？

lines.speaker: Q23.
lines.text: What had probably caused humans to lose most of their hair one and a half million years ago?
lines.translation: 大约150万年前，可能是什么原因导致人类失去了大部分毛发？

lines.speaker: Q24.
lines.text: What does the speaker say protected early humans from the sun?
lines.translation: 发言者说是什么保护了早期人类免受阳光照射？

lines.speaker: Q25.
lines.text: What happened when early humans left Africa to populate the rest of the world?
lines.translation: 当早期人类离开非洲到世界其他地方定居时，发生了什么？
